reviews.text: Charles D Naylor and Joe Rinaldi attorneys represent my brother in a Maritime law case. Recently, they came to Europe to take deposition from a witness in Linz, Austria, and 4 witnesses in Budapest and Szentendre, Hungary. The weather was very cold. Huge pieces of ice were flowing down river Danube, it was snowing non-stop. The roads were slippery and the side walks were covered with ice. The temperature was minus 12 Celsius degrees. reviews.ownerResponse:
While we are unable to disclose confidential information about your husband’s case, we can respond to your false accusations. First, our firm did not charge $15,000.00 for legal representation. We did not charge even half of that in legal fees. The “second” process, you discuss in your review was being done on a pro bono basis as our firm handles approximately four pro bono matters per year. Second, throughout the representation, our office has been in communication with you & your husband about his case. At least the case notes indicate this to be true. As far as “follow-through,” you know (because we met & review the applications) that the matter was ready for filing. However, when you called our office & told us what happened between you & your husband, a conflict of interest arose. This was explained to you. We understand that you wanted us to continue the representation but doing so, in our opinion, would jeopardize our license. To be certain we could no longer represent your husband or you, we contacted the State Bar of California Ethics Committee & were told we MUST withdraw our representation immediately & could not communicate with either of you again as it could create an attorney/client relationship.

Again, while we are unable to disclose confidential information about your niece’s case, we can respond to your false accusations. As you are aware because we discussed the following, when I appeared at your niece’s & her son’s Master Hearing, all of the documents were filed & an Individual (AKA trial) Hearing was scheduled. I never heard from her or you since the Master Hearing despite many attempts to reach her to prepare for trial. The Immigration Judge agreed that we made enough efforts & granted our Motion to Withdraw. She was provided with a letter explaining what happened together with other documents to help her find new counsel.

We are not sure why you believe we do not have immigration experience or knowledge. We pride ourselves in explaining the process thoroughly to all of our clients & did the same with you (as the authorized person to speak to), several times throughout your husband & niece’s representations. We are sorry the attorney/client relationship did not work out with your husband & niece but there are many immigration attorneys in Southern California that are competent to handle their immigration matter.
We're sorry you cancelled your appointment due to our not validating parking and charging for the consultation.  We wish you luck in your immigration matter.

BEWARE!!! This Is a Predatory “Law Firm” Built on Extracting Consultation Fees

This operation is not a tenant defense law firm, it’s a consultation fee mill that preys on people in crisis. The consultation fee is the real product here, not legal representation.

I contacted them for help with an unlawful detainer involving my senior parents. From the moment the phone was answered, the red flags were obvious. Cesar, the intake guy, talks to you as if he’s the attorney, confidently promising victories and “strong defenses” before anyone has even reviewed a single document. He immediately pushed the $250 consultation fee and a $2,500 retainer, assuring me the consultation would be applied toward representation.

I uploaded all documents as instructed, including serious defenses like:
•	A city citation against the landlord for habitability violations
•	Fraudulent inducement
•	Unlicensed individuals executing the lease
•	Multiple safety and habitability issues
When the actual attorney finally called (late), he hadn’t reviewed anything. Not the documents. Not the defenses. Not the city citation. Nothing. His entire “analysis” was a blanket statement that my parents would lose because “a judge wouldn’t even consider” their defenses. No explanation. No strategy. No effort.

Then came the real punchline:
The retainer wasn’t $2,500, it was $2,500 + $250 per person + $2,000 per month until the case ended. And for what? This attorney didn’t propose:
•	Discovery
•	A counterclaim
•	A demurrer
•	ANY actual defense strategy

He didn’t even pretend to care.
For contrast:

I hired a legitimate eviction defense service for $500. They reviewed the documents, filed a demurrer, and got the case dismissed. Something this “law firm” never even mentioned.

If they had any shame, they'd return my parents' money.

I contacted the email listed in the owner’s reply. They never responded. Not even a basic acknowledgment.
Their public response suggests they’re willing to address concerns, but in reality, they ignored my outreach entirely. This confirms the pattern I described in my review.

This misdemeanor conviction had some serious immigration liabilities for my Godson. The only way to remove this conviction was to put a motion before the Judge to get the original conviction overturned or dismissed by the court.

Leonard explained that there would be several roadblocks to overcome in a case like this. Generally, prosecutors and Judges do not like to go back and dismiss a conviction. So knowing it would be a challenge I believed Leonard would do his best.

And he did, with Leonard’s legal knowledge and strategy along with all the good and positive contributions documented about my Godson giving back to our community. The motion was put together by Leonard.
In the end, Leonard presented the motion to dismiss to the Lead Prosecutor and they did not block it. I was in the court last week when Leonard Matsuk addressed the motion before the judge, he answered the judge’s questions. The prosecutor advised the judge they were not blocking the motion.

Quite a crowd of onlookers gathered on that November evening in 2015 to watch my arrest after failing to accept the need for breathalyzer testing which had been demanded arrogantly, I felt, by the "officer in charge". I had already failed the attitude test miserably, you know the one where you are supposed to become a wimpy submissive girlie man based on the authoritarian tone of said OIC. After asking what my options were in lieu of testing and being informed that I could simply surrender my license for a couple of years I chose that option. I told the officious OIC in question that I could afford a driver, no worries.

I was then immediately arrested for public drunkenness. This puff chested little dweeb refused to answer when asked if it was because my car cost more than his annual salary. No girlie man here, so off to the Long Beach jail I went. Sometime later I offered to take the test, which I was unable to pass, failing in fact by a very small margin. Leonard informed me, to my amazement, that blood alcohol levels continue to climb for more than an hour after your last drink and I probably would have passed if I had complied with the officer's request when initially pulled over. But what the heck, are we mice or men?

I knew I was in danger of spending 10 grand if convicted, plus loss of license, restrictions, insurance rate hikes, as well as the social stigma of being a drunken killer loose on the highway. The more I thought about it the worse it became. My anxiety was approaching the need for a couple of weeks of restorative work on the psych ward of my choosing. I made inquiries with trusted friends, a couple of whom suggested Leonard Matsuk.  A call was placed, an appointment arranged, and I began to feel a little better.

Leonard's approach was direct and straight forward. He laid out plans for both fighting the offense in court and the possibility of a reduced charge. He, and his staff, were quick to return calls and provide quotes for all facets of my defense. Eventually all was decided in court exactly as he had anticipated on our first meeting, the outcome my now well informed self was also expecting. It was uncanny how Leonard predicted what prosecuting attorneys would do at every turn, meeting them prepared and confident. I felt I was very well represented and was provided timely and factual information as to how the courts operated.  Leonard even offered to include a defense at a DMV hearing at no additional charge. He appeared, again at no additional charge, on a ticket issued 30 days after the 1st matter for driving in the initial 30 day restriction: all praise to our ever efficient DMV.   I mixed up the dates. The charges were (1) driving on a restricted license, and (2) speeding, 82 mph on the 605. Definitely driving license suspension time and perhaps a little jail time to awaken the idiot within.  All charges were DISMISSED.

Regardless of your situation, innocent as a lamb (the story you tell your wife), or guilty as sin (the story you tell your attorney, Leonard), DO NOT GO TO COURT UNREPRESENTED. Only a complete wanker shows up without an attorney if there is even the remotest possibility of even one night in jail. Don't do it. Call Leonard.

My experience is similar to those who referred me on to Leonard's practice. We all needed a knowledgeable lawyer of integrity, of strong character, of substantial experience in the local courts, a man who wouldn't require us to hock our gold teeth for settlement of his charges, and importantly, a man of the world experienced enough to use every last loophole, trick, and guise known to mankind.  I'm a little older than he, mid 60s as of now, and I've seen my share of the belly of the beast if you get my drift.  Leonard knows the drill, period!

If you need to understand your OPTIONS and want the WORRIES about your grim future TO DISSAPEAR, if you need an ear biting Mohammed Ali type heavy weight in your corner, and you're looking for a superior representation in the South land courts at affordable rates, believe me, Leonard Matsuk is your Man.

Cheers,

I worked with Agata from 2019 to 2021 as she helped with my family law case.

She is a good person who means well. When we spoke on the phone, she always took the necessary time to explain her professional opinion and the possible view from the court's perspective. She exemplified caring about my situation and I believe that Agata provided me with her best advice for the situation. Where she really shines is litigation. On the occasions we went to court, she definitely threw the gloves on and fought for the case.

Unfortunately, no attorney is perfect. There were some shortfalls within my experience:

1. Administrative - To my knowledge, there was no paralegal employed in the office. There are a lot of moving parts to each case and I felt Agata was a little over her head. The time it took to get a problem resolved took longer than expected due to this. There was an instance where Agata followed up with the opposing party, only for everyone to realize she had not even sent the e-mail for the opposing party to actually respond to.

2. Communication - For the most part, Agata replied within 24 hours when I would leave a voicemail. However, there were numerous occasions where I did not hear back for days. While completely understandable that personal issues may arise, the lack of communication was noted as a considerable weakness, especially when clients and opposing parties are waiting on hearing back from your lawyer to move forward. I wasn't notified all the time if she received my emails. If she replied that she would do something by a certain date, a lot of the time the deadline was not met. I had to follow up with Agata on multiple occasions to get an update on my case, so it was difficult to know if anything had been done. It came to a point where I had to state that I was not paying for follow up emails. COVID aside, my case dragged on partly due to this factor.

3. Limited Proactiveness - While Agata was proactive in fighting for me during litigation, she was not as strong in being proactive with solutions outside the courtroom. As with any family law case, it's a stressful and arduous experience for everyone. A good lawyer makes you feel like someone has your back through this tough time. Unfortunately, this was not the case in my opinion and how I felt overall.

Agata is a kind person who means well. If you're looking for someone with a strong litigation background and think you may go to court, Agata may be a good fit. If you have expectations for strong communication and exceptional administrative qualities, keep interviewing lawyers.

reviews.text:
My worse nightmare came to life when I became a victim of police abuse of power. Everything seemed hopeless from the misrepresented Police Report to the lack of response from Police Department and finally the skewed Judicial System weighing the evidence from the marred case presented. It felt impossible to have the truth be heard, “I am Innocent”, among the deceitful crowd I was now being shuffled among.
It was like a movie, the dirty cops, the crooked internal affairs and the one attorney who fights for the truth. Except, this wasn't a movie, it was my life.
I hired Al within my first consultation. Although I had called and spoken to other lawyers, his calmness and collectiveness made me feel for the first time, someone is listening. He didn't tell me what I wanted to hear, he told me the way it was. His years as a Prosecutor gave me insightful understanding to how the DA views my case and how we can prepare our defense.  Al had extensive knowledge of my court district and the judges as well as the prosecutors. He came highly recommended and as Neil said, “If Al can’t do it, it can’t be done”.  I left his office feeling that I had made the best decision and investment for my life.
Al was always there for me day and night. He provided me with his office, cell, assistant office and assistant cell number so I can reach him at any hour of the day, believe me, I called and texted at all hours.  When I couldn't reach him, his assistant Jill was ever vigilant with keeping me informed.  Al always kept me informed of everything he was doing and how he was going to do it.  He always told me what angles were available for me to approach and win my case. I always had options and felt that he was there to for fight for me.  He always had my well being in mind first and foremost.  Even when I felt low he was there to pick me up, not as a lawyer, but as a friend, and told me to hang in there; there’s still a win for me.
It was a tough fight. Al fought for me against the Long Beach Police Department, Long Beach Internal Affairs and Long Beach District Attorney’s office. Even when ever Motion and Order was not heard, Al never faltered or staggered in my defense, instead, he dug his heels in deeper. He fought for justice and my truth to be heard. It was his years of experience, skills and abilities that opened the communication barrier to the Prosecutors to show, I was Entrapped and Innocent of any charges.  He fought the Good Fight and that is why my case was DISMISSED.
Not even an infraction, not a fine, not anything. I won, and I owe it all to Al! His perseverance and efforts allowed my innocence to be heard and a case that otherwise would have be pleaded to, was a DISMISSAL due to the effective execution of this phenomenal lawyer Al F. Amer.
